args:
Benchmark task: routing-read-write-ledger
Repository: https://github.com/honojs/hono.git

Create a new file at bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md. This is a read/write endurance task, not a clever debugging task. First create the bench-artifacts directory if it does not exist. Then read every file in this exact list before writing the final document: src/hono.ts, src/hono-base.ts, src/router/pattern-router/index.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.ts, src/router/linear-router/index.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts, src/router/smart-router/index.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.ts, src/router/trie-router/index.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.ts, src/router/common.case.test.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts. The output file must be long but mechanically simple. Start with a title '# Routing Read/Write Ledger'. For each listed file, add a section whose heading is exactly '## <file path>'. Under each heading write three bullets: '- Purpose: ...', '- Names noticed: ...', and '- Simple note: ...'. Keep the notes plain and safe; do not change source code. After the per-file sections, add '## Cross-File Checklist' with one bullet per router family: pattern-router, linear-router, reg-exp-router, smart-router, trie-router. Include exact file paths in the final answer and report that only bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md was created.

Constraints:
- Do not ask the user to write prompts or inspect files manually.
- Prefer the smallest safe code change when edits are required.
- Include exact file paths, commands run, and verification status in the final answer.

artifacts.content:
# Routing Read/Write Ledger

This document records the read/write ledger for the routing module of Hono.

---

## src/hono.ts

- Purpose: Main entry point for the Hono framework, defining the Hono class that extends HonoBase for routing and custom options.
- Names noticed: `Hono`, `HonoBase`, `BasePath`
- Simple note: Entry point that sets up routing and allows custom options to be passed to the Hono instance.

---

## src/hono-base.ts

- Purpose: Base module for the Hono object containing the core Hono class implementation, middleware handling, and routing setup.
- Names noticed: `HonoBase`, `HonoOptions`, `ErrorHandler`, `NotFoundHandler`, `HandlerInterface`, `MiddlewareHandler`, `CurrentPath`, `BasePath`
- Simple note: Contains the foundational HonoBase class with middleware support, strict mode, custom router option handling, and base routing logic.

---

## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export for the PatternRouter class.
- Names noticed: `PatternRouter`
- Simple note: Index file that exports the PatternRouter implementation from router.ts.

---

## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements a pattern-based routing approach using regular expressions compiled from route paths for matching.
- Names noticed: `PatternRouter`, `Route`, `RegExp`, `match`, `exec`, `routes`, `handler`
- Simple note: Routes are stored as regular expressions, and matching is done by executing each pattern against the incoming path. Allows trailing slashes.

---

## src/router/linear-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export for the LinearRouter class.
- Names noticed: `LinearRouter`
- Simple note: Index file that exports the LinearRouter implementation from router.ts.

---

## src/router/linear-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements a linear tree-based routing approach that walks the path sequentially to find matching handlers.
- Names noticed: `LinearRouter`, `Router`, `match`, `add`, `stack`, `paramIndex`, `children`, `handleChildren`, `wildcards`
- Simple note: Builds a nested tree structure where paths are matched by walking down branches. Supports wildcards and parameter matching with sequential traversal.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export for the RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter.
- Names noticed: `RegExpRouter`, `PreparedRegExpRouter`, `buildInitParams`, `serializeInitParams`
- Simple note: Exports the RegExpRouter class for runtime pattern matching and a Prepared variant for pre-built matchers.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Core RegExpRouter that pre-builds a single regex pattern from all routes for fast matching.
- Names noticed: `RegExpRouter`, `Matcher`, `buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes`, `wildcardRegExpCache`, `buildRegExp`, `staticMap`, `HandlerWithMetadata`
- Simple note: All routes are compiled into a single regular expression with capture groups for parameters. Static routes are cached separately for O(1) lookup.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts

- Purpose: Defines matcher types and matching logic for the RegExpRouter.
- Names noticed: `Matcher`, `MatcherMap`, `HandlerData`, `StaticMap`, `ParamIndexMap`, `ParamStash`, `match`, `emptyParam`
- Simple note: Matcher is a tuple of [RegExp, HandlerData[], StaticMap]. Matching returns handler index and param stash.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts

- Purpose: Provides a PreparedRegExpRouter that pre-builds matchers at initialization for faster runtime matching.
- Names noticed: `PreparedRegExpRouter`, `buildAndExportAllMatchers`, `buildMatchers`, `#addPath`, `#addWildcard`, `serializeInitParams`
- Simple note: Routes are added at initialization time, build all matchers upfront, and export them as JSON for serverless/deployment scenarios.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Defines the Node class for building the trie structure used by RegExpRouter.
- Names noticed: `Node`, `insert`, `buildRegExpStr`, `#children`, `LABEL_REG_EXP_STR`, `varIndex`, `paramMap`
- Simple note: Nodes represent trie entries with regexp tokens or literal strings. Builds a regexp string for the entire route by concatenating regexp segments.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts

- Purpose: Implements the Trie data structure for organizing routes before building the final regex.
- Names noticed: `Trie`, `buildRegExp`, `#root`, `ParamAssocArray`, `context`, `ReplacementMap`
- Simple note: Trie organizes routes by path prefix, then builds a single regex with special markers (# for handlers, @ for params) that are replaced during final compilation.

---

## src/router/smart-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export for the SmartRouter class.
- Names noticed: `SmartRouter`
- Simple note: Index file that exports the SmartRouter implementation from router.ts.

---

## src/router/smart-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements a composition router that wraps and delegates to another router, determining the best fit at runtime.
- Names noticed: `SmartRouter`, `routers`, `activeRouter`, `#routers`, `#routes`, `buildAllMatchers`, `matchers`
- Simple note: Wraps another router (RegExpRouter or TrieRouter) and provides a unified interface. Name shows which underlying router is active.

---

## src/router/trie-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export for the TrieRouter class.
- Names noticed: `TrieRouter`
- Simple note: Index file that exports the TrieRouter implementation from router.ts.

---

## src/router/trie-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements the TrieRouter class that delegates to an underlying Node-based trie router.
- Names noticed: `TrieRouter`, `Trie`
- Simple note: Minimal wrapper around the Trie class, providing Router interface implementation. Name and router name are set.

---

## src/router/trie-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements the Node class with insert and search methods for prefix-matching based routing.
- Names noticed: `Node`, `insert`, `search`, `#children`, `#varIndex`, `#methodStack`, `METHOD_NAME_ALL`, `ParamIndexMap`
- Simple note: Each node tracks methods in a stack and wildcards. Search walks the path by matching segments. Supports optional parameters and method-specific handlers.

---

## src/router/common.case.test.ts

- Purpose: Shared test utilities and common test cases for all routers.
- Names noticed: `runTest`, `describe`, `test`, `getSuiteHierarchy`, `Router`, `match`, `add`, `matcher`, `params`, `stash`
- Simple note: Provides a generic `runTest` function that takes a router constructor and test suite name, then runs common routing scenarios (basic routes, params, wildcards, etc.).

---

## src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for PatternRouter including shared tests and pattern-specific cases.
- Names noticed: `PatternRouter`, `runTest`, `skip`, `UnsupportedPathError`, `Duplicate param name`, `Trailing slash`
- Simple note: Uses common.test.ts framework, skips tests for duplicate params and certain edge cases. PatternRouter allows trailing slashes.

---

## src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for LinearRouter including shared tests and linear-specific cases.
- Names noticed: `LinearRouter`, `runTest`, `skip`, `Wildcard stack`, `Wildcard in segment`, `Trailing slash`, `Custom regexp`
- Simple note: Uses common.test.ts framework, skips wildcard stack and segment tests. Supports custom regexps like `:id{d+}`.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for RegExpRouter including shared tests and regexp-specific cases.
- Names noticed: `RegExpRouter`, `runTest`, `skip`, `Ambiguous path pattern`, `Capture complex directories`, `Ambiguous static path`
- Simple note: Uses common.test.ts framework, skips ambiguous patterns and includes complex multi-segment captures. Prebuilds regex at initialization.

---

## src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for SmartRouter delegating to RegExpRouter and TrieRouter.
- Names noticed: `SmartRouter`, `RegExpRouter`, `TrieRouter`, `runTest`
- Simple note: Simple test wrapping common cases with SmartRouter + RegExpRouter combo.

---

## src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for TrieRouter using shared common test cases.
- Names noticed: `TrieRouter`, `runTest`
- Simple note: Minimal test file using the common test framework with TrieRouter.

---

## src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts

- Purpose: Comprehensive unit tests for the Node class with many specific routing scenarios.
- Names noticed: `Node`, `search`, `insert`, `get`, `post`, `delete`, `all`, `optional`, `wildcard`, `regex`, `params`, `paramIndex`
- Simple note: Contains many individual test cases testing path matching, parameter extraction, optional segments, wildcards, regex patterns, and method-specific routing. Tests cover edge cases like empty paths, duplicate params, and complex nested structures.

---

## Cross-File Checklist

### pattern-router
- src/router/pattern-router/index.ts
- src/router/pattern-router/router.ts
- src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts
- src/router/common.case.test.ts

### linear-router
- src/router/linear-router/index.ts
- src/router/linear-router/router.ts
- src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts
- src/router/common.case.test.ts

### reg-exp-router
- src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts
- src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts
- src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts
- src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts
- src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts
- src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts
- src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts
- src/router/common.case.test.ts

### smart-router
- src/router/smart-router/index.ts
- src/router/smart-router/router.ts
- src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts
- src/router/common.case.test.ts

### trie-router
- src/router/trie-router/index.ts
- src/router/trie-router/router.ts
- src/router/trie-router/node.ts
- src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts
- src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts
- src/router/common.case.test.ts

---

**Files Created:** `bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md`
**Verification Status:** File created successfully at bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md.
